project_overview.project_name: Second Ethiopia Resilient Landscapes and Livelihoods Project (RLLP II)
project_overview.project_development_objective: To improve climate resilience, land productivity and carbon storage, and increase access to diversified livelihood activities in selected rural watersheds
project_overview.total_financing: US$178.24 million (US$165.24 million from Green Climate Fund, US$13.0 million from PROGREEN)
project_overview.beneficiaries: 706,189 people (138,311 households) in 47 rural watersheds, primarily vulnerable smallholder farmers

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - Tigray Region under conflict situation with paused WBG activities, newly formed Sidama Region with establishing governance structures

key_components.description: Land restoration through sustainable land management, climate smart agriculture, and livelihood diversification including value chain connections

key_components.component_name: Investing in Institutions and Information for Resilience
key_components.description: Capacity building at local government level, information modernization, establishment of Community Watershed Users' Cooperative Societies
Project management, fiduciary aspects, monitoring and evaluation, and reporting

implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: National and Regional Steering Committees, National and Regional Technical Committees, Project Coordination Units at federal and regional levels
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF, mix of international and national competitive bidding, community-based procurement
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Quarterly reporting, annual performance reports to GCF, remote monitoring tools including UAVs and electronic tablets

identified_risks.risk_description: Sporadic civil unrest in project areas, conflict situation in Tigray Region
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Careful implementation support mission planning emphasizing security, strategic communication, sound safeguards monitoring, enhanced transparency

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited human resources at field level, weak coordination among institutions, challenge of implementing reliable M&E system
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Continual training on project management, enhanced technical coordination, execution of MoUs clarifying accountability

identified_risks.risk_description: Delay in budget preparation, low budget utilization, weak tracking and reporting, risk of expenditure comingling between projects
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Detailed FM assessment, action plan implementation, separate accounting systems, stringent control measures

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited capacity at local levels for risk identification, potential invasive species introduction, occupational health and safety issues
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Capacity building at all levels, implementation of ESMF, stakeholder engagement, grievance redress mechanisms

identified_risks.risk_description: Weak multi-sectoral coordination, potential elite capture of project benefits, exclusion of underserved community members
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Intersectoral coordination mechanisms, communication measures, participatory development, enhanced transparency

identified_risks.risk_description: COVID-19 pandemic impacts on implementation pace and local markets
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Enhanced remote monitoring, remote supervision tools, acceleration of income-generating activities, health guidelines implementation

triggered_safeguard_policies: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - project-level GRM established with committees at various levels, World Bank Grievance Redress Service available
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Gender Action Plan implemented, women's participation required in watershed teams, joint land certificates for married couples, targeted support for women-headed households

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project designed to alleviate drivers of civil unrest including natural resource degradation and rural joblessness, careful security planning for implementation missions
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Ethiopia faces climate vulnerability, land degradation affecting millions, recurring droughts and floods, locust outbreaks, newly formed regional governments with establishing capacity
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: WBG activities paused in Tigray Region due to conflict, adaptive measures required for both Tigray and newly formed Sidama Region, security-conscious implementation planning
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: no specific displacement focus, but project targets vulnerable rural populations and aims to build resilience against climate-induced displacement risks
